data.title: As the DSO workflow, I can defend a design decision by posting a PR review comment and replying to the original comment thread
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Orchestrator logic for the defend action handler: receives a pre-classified defend comment along with a defense argument generated by the classification step, posts a new PR review comment (not an inline source code comment) with the justification, and replies to the original thread linking to that review comment.
**Why**: Engineers deserve a substantive response when the workflow maintains a design decision. A PR review comment is the appropriate venue for design justifications — it keeps source code clean and surfaces the rationale in the PR conversation.
**Scope**:
- IN: Receive a pre-classified defend comment with generated defense argument text (produced by Story 5's classification prompt — not generated here); post a new PR review comment via the correct endpoint: inline (is_inline=true) → POST /pulls/{n}/comments/{root_comment_id}/replies; top-level (is_inline=false) → POST /issues/{n}/comments; write the returned comment ID to defense_comment_id in the normalized JSON file BEFORE posting the thread reply; use the shared reply-posting function from Story 1 to post a thread reply linking to the posted review comment; retry idempotency: if defense_comment_id is already non-null in the JSON, skip step 1 (post review comment) and proceed directly to step 2 (post thread reply); fallback: if inline reply returns 422, fall back to POST /issues/{n}/comments; all reply bodies begin with <!-- dso-agent-reply -->
- OUT: Defense argument text generation (Story 5's classification prompt produces this); source code modification; REVIEW-DEFENSE: inline source code comments; comment classification

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a defend-classified comment results in a new PR review comment posted via the correct GitHub API endpoint based on the is_inline field in the normalized JSON
  ← Satisfies: "post a new PR review comment (not a # REVIEW-DEFENSE: source code comment) via the correct endpoint (inline: POST /pulls/{n}/comments/{root_comment_id}/replies; top-level: POST /issues/{n}/comments)" (SC3)
- When this story is complete, the returned review comment ID is written to defense_comment_id in the normalized JSON file before the thread reply is posted
  ← Satisfies: "write the returned comment ID to defense_comment_id in the normalized JSON" (SC3)
- When this story is complete, if defense_comment_id is already non-null (retry case), step 1 (post review comment) is skipped and only the thread reply is posted
  ← Satisfies: "on retry, defense_comment_id being non-null skips step 1 and proceeds directly to step 2" (SC3)
- When this story is complete, if the inline reply endpoint returns 422, the handler falls back to POST /issues/{n}/comments without failing
  ← Satisfies: "Fallback to POST /issues/{n}/comments when inline reply returns 422" (epic Scenario Analysis)
- When this story is complete, the thread reply links to the posted review comment and begins with <!-- dso-agent-reply -->
  ← Satisfies: "reply to the originating thread linking to that review comment; all reply bodies begin with <!-- dso-agent-reply -->" (SC3)
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic
  ← TDD Done-of-Done requirement

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Two-step defend is non-atomic — defense_comment_id persistence after step 1 before step 2 is the sole idempotency mechanism; test must cover the case where step 2 fails and retry correctly skips step 1
- [Reliability] Comment on a deleted file may return 422 from the inline reply endpoint — fallback to POST /issues/{n}/comments is required; test must cover the 422 fallback path
- [Testing] Test must cover both is_inline=true and is_inline=false endpoint routing paths independently
- [Maintainability] Use the shared thread-reply posting function from Story 1 for the thread reply call — do not reimplement is_inline endpoint routing for the thread reply step

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
